headline: Amazon Bedrock expands to New Zealand, enhancing AI accessibility in Asia Pacific.

items.title: Amazon Bedrock expands to New Zealand
items.summary: Amazon Bedrock is now available in the Asia Pacific (New Zealand) Region, providing access to various models including Anthropic's Claude Opus and Amazon's Nova. This expansion allows local customers to integrate advanced AI capabilities into their applications, offering new opportunities for innovation and efficiency within the region.
items.why_it_matters: This expansion supports developers in New Zealand with direct access to powerful AI models, enabling the creation of more sophisticated AI applications.

items.title: Introducing Amazon Polly Bidirectional Streaming
items.summary: Amazon Polly's new Bidirectional Streaming API allows real-time text-to-speech synthesis, enabling developers to send text and receive audio concurrently. This functionality supports the development of interactive conversational AI applications, ensuring smoother user experiences.

items.title: Build with Lyria 3, Google's new music generation model
items.summary: Google's Lyria 3, a music generation model, is now available for developers. It allows the creation of longer music tracks with improved structural awareness, expanding the possibilities for integrating AI-generated music into various Google products.

items.title: Accelerating LLM fine-tuning with AWS SageMaker
items.summary: AWS has enhanced its SageMaker Unified Studio with Amazon S3, simplifying the use of unstructured data for machine learning. This integration streamlines the process of fine-tuning large language models, optimizing workflows for developers handling extensive datasets.

items.title: Anthropic Subprocessor Changes
items.summary: Anthropic has announced changes to its subprocessors, impacting how data is managed and processed within its AI services. These changes may affect compliance and integration strategies for developers using Anthropic's AI solutions.

items.title: ARC-AGI-3: A New Challenge for Frontier Agentic Intelligence
items.summary: A novel benchmark, ARC-AGI-3, has been introduced for studying agentic intelligence. This benchmark involves turn-based environments requiring agents to infer goals and plan actions, offering insights into developing more autonomous AI systems.

all_items.title: Run Generative AI inference with Amazon Bedrock in Asia Pacific (New Zealand)
all_items.summary: Today, we’re excited to announce that Amazon Bedrock is now available in the Asia Pacific (New Zealand) Region (ap-southeast-6). Customers in New Zealand can now access Anthropic Claude models (Claude Opus 4.5, Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.5, Sonnet 4.6, and Haiku 4.5) and Amazon (Nova 2 Lite) models directly in the Auckland Region with cross region inference. all_items.title: Building age-responsive, context-aware AI with Amazon Bedrock Guardrails
all_items.summary: In this post, we walk you through how to implement a fully automated, context-aware AI solution using a serverless architecture on AWS. This solution helps organizations looking to deploy responsible AI systems, align with compliance requirements for vulnerable populations, and help maintain appropriate and trustworthy AI responses across diverse user groups without compromising performance or governance.

all_items.title: Accelerating LLM fine-tuning with unstructured data using SageMaker Unified Studio and S3
all_items.summary: Last year, AWS announced an integration between Amazon SageMaker Unified Studio and Amazon S3 general purpose buckets. This integration makes it straightforward for teams to use unstructured data stored in Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) for machine learning (ML) and data analytics use cases. In this post, we show how to integrate S3 general purpose buckets with Amazon SageMaker Catalog to fine-tune Llama 3.2 11B Vision Instruct for visual question answering (VQA) using Amazon SageMake

all_items.title: Introducing Amazon Polly Bidirectional Streaming: Real-time speech synthesis for conversational AI
all_items.summary: Today, we’re excited to announce the new Bidirectional Streaming API for Amazon Polly, enabling streamlined real-time text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis where you can start sending text and receiving audio simultaneously. This new API is built for conversational AI applications that generate text or audio incrementally, like responses from large language models (LLMs), where users must begin synthesizing audio before the full text is available.

all_items.title: Unlocking video insights at scale with Amazon Bedrock multimodal models
all_items.summary: In this post, we explore how the multimodal foundation models (FMs) of Amazon Bedrock enable scalable video understanding through three distinct architectural approaches. Each approach is designed for different use cases and cost-performance trade-offs.

all_items.title: Deploy voice agents with Pipecat and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime – Part 1
all_items.summary: In this series of posts, you will learn how streaming architectures help address these challenges using Pipecat voice agents on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime. In Part 1, you will learn how to deploy Pipecat voice agents on AgentCore Runtime using different network transport approaches including WebSockets, WebRTC and telephony integration, with practical deployment guidance and code samples.

all_items.summary: arXiv:2603.24800v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: In this paper, we uncover the hidden potential of Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) to significantly enhance generative tasks.
